candidates.title: The Absorption Tax: How Feature Hierarchy Structure Governs SAE Failure Modes Across Semantic Domains

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders. arXiv:2409.14507 (NeurIPS 2025)
collisions.overlap: Defines absorption metric and measures it, but exclusively on first-letter spelling task. Does not extend to entity-attribute hierarchies.

collisions.paper: Karvonen et al., 2025. SAEBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Sparse Autoencoders. arXiv:2503.09532 (ICML 2025)
collisions.overlap: Includes absorption as benchmark metric, but only first-letter task

collisions.paper: Chaudhary & Geiger, 2024. Evaluating Open-Source SAEs on Disentangling Factual Knowledge in GPT-2 Small. arXiv:2409.04478
collisions.overlap: Uses RAVEL for SAE evaluation on city-country/continent, but measures disentanglement (causal intervention success), NOT absorption (parent feature false negative rate). Fundamentally different metric.

collisions.paper: SynthSAEBench, 2026. arXiv:2602.14687
collisions.overlap: Evaluates SAEs on synthetic hierarchical features including absorption, but synthetic data not real LLM knowledge hierarchies

collisions.paper: Muchane et al., 2025. Incorporating Hierarchical Semantics in SAE Architectures. arXiv:2506.01197
collisions.overlap: Models semantic hierarchy in SAE architecture. Focused on architecture, not absorption measurement.

collisions.paper: Narayanaswamy et al., 2026. Improving Robustness In Sparse Autoencoders via Masked Regularization. arXiv:2604.06495
collisions.overlap: Proposes masking-based regularization to disrupt co-occurrence patterns. Mitigation method, not characterization.

collisions.paper: Resurrecting the Salmon, 2025. arXiv:2508.09363
collisions.overlap: Notes SAEBench first-letter metric is insufficient for domain-specific evaluation. Identifies gap but does not fill it.

sub_contributions.differentiation_notes: RAVEL has been used for disentanglement evaluation but never for absorption measurement. The absorption metric has only been applied to first-letter spelling. This is the cleanest novelty claim. The pilot finding that first-letter is atypical (LOWEST absorption) adds unexpected value.

sub_contributions.contribution: Causal absorption via activation patching (SECONDARY, NEW in Round 5)

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption. arXiv:2409.14507
collisions.overlap: Uses integrated gradients (correlational attribution) and ablation to measure downstream effect. Does NOT zero child to measure parent recovery -- different experimental design.

collisions.paper: Marks et al., 2024. Sparse Feature Circuits. arXiv:2403.19647
collisions.overlap: Uses activation patching on SAE features for general circuit discovery. Not absorption-specific parent-child recovery.

collisions.paper: Dunefsky et al., 2024. Transcoders Find Interpretable LLM Feature Circuits. NeurIPS 2024
collisions.overlap: Uses causal patching between SAE features. Transcoder context, not absorption-specific.

collisions.paper: VisualScratchpad, 2026. arXiv:2603.07335
collisions.overlap: Notes hierarchical SAE latents complicate ablation. Identifies issue but does not use it to study absorption mechanism.

sub_contributions.differentiation_notes: First interventional (not correlational) evidence for competitive exclusion in SAEs. Chanin et al. use integrated gradients; this uses activation patching (zero child, measure parent recovery). Analogous to observational vs. experimental distinction in causal inference.

sub_contributions.contribution: Tightened hedging classification (SECONDARY, NEW in Round 5)

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2025. Feature Hedging. arXiv:2505.11756
collisions.overlap: Defines hedging and provides classification criteria. Does not critique or tighten the classification methodology.

collisions.paper: Chanin & Garriga-Alonso, 2025. Sparse but Wrong. arXiv:2508.16560
collisions.overlap: Shows L0 confound. Does not analyze hedging classification threshold.

sub_contributions.differentiation_notes: The widely cited 98.6% hedging figure is near-tautological. Strict classification yields 7.4% with 85.3% compensatory resolution. No prior work critiques this classification.

collisions.paper: Chanin et al., 2024. A is for Absorption. arXiv:2409.14507
collisions.overlap: Notes informally that absorption saves +1 L0 per parent-child pair. Qualitative observation only.

collisions.paper: Tang et al., 2025/2026. Unified Theory of SDL. arXiv:2512.05534
collisions.overlap: Characterizes absorption as spurious partial minima. Does NOT quantify cost of elimination or derive impossibility bounds.

collisions.paper: Ayonrinde et al., 2024. MDL-SAEs. arXiv:2410.11179
collisions.overlap: Rate-distortion framework for SAEs. Does not analyze absorption-elimination cost.

collisions.paper: Cui et al., 2025. On the Limits of SAEs. arXiv:2506.15963
collisions.overlap: Related impossibility result but different formulation (feature recovery vs. absorption cost).

sub_contributions.differentiation_notes: Formal impossibility result is novel but pilot evidence shows quantitative predictions are weak (absorption-MSE rho=0.08, R_pc rho=0.16). Report as qualitative framework only. Downgraded from 8/10 to 7/10.

collisions.paper: Karvonen et al., 2025. SAEBench. arXiv:2503.09532
collisions.overlap: Compares architectures on absorption, but only first-letter task

collisions.paper: Bussmann et al., 2025. Matryoshka SAEs. arXiv:2503.17547
collisions.overlap: Shows Matryoshka beats others on absorption (first-letter). Single task.

collisions.paper: SynthSAEBench, 2026. arXiv:2602.14687
collisions.overlap: Compares architectures on synthetic data. Not real knowledge hierarchies.

sub_contributions.differentiation_notes: No prior work compares absorption architecture rankings across multiple real knowledge hierarchy types. JumpReLU consistently lowest is a surprising finding that contradicts Matryoshka's assumed superiority.

negative_results.detail: rho=0.12, well below 0.3 failure threshold. Decoder-activation mismatch does not capture absorption.
rho=0.044, p=0.83. Information-theoretic pillar does not hold.

negative_results.result: H2 FALSIFIED: First-letter is NOT worst case
negative_results.detail: First-letter shows 3.9% absorption (lowest). This is actually a positive finding -- the inversion is more interesting.

candidates.differentiation_notes: All primary contributions are genuinely novel. Cross-domain characterization (9/10) is the cleanest claim. Causal patching (8/10) provides first interventional evidence. Tightened hedging (8/10) is independently valuable. Absorption Tax (7/10) downgraded but still novel as qualitative framework. Architecture comparison (7/10) is a natural extension. No competing cross-domain absorption characterization exists as of April 2026.

candidates.title: Controlled Dictionary Experiment: Separating Encoder from Dictionary in Absorption Causation

collisions.paper: Costa et al., 2025. Evaluating SAEs: From Shallow Design to Matching Pursuit. arXiv:2506.05239
collisions.overlap: Uses matching pursuit as alternative encoder. Jointly trains encoder and dictionary, does not hold dictionary constant.

collisions.paper: Stop Probing, Start Coding, 2026. arXiv:2603.28744
collisions.overlap: Compares FISTA, LISTA, and Matching Pursuit inference strategies. Different inference algorithms but not controlled dictionary experiment.

collisions.paper: SynthSAEBench, 2026. arXiv:2602.14687
collisions.overlap: Evaluates MP-SAE. No explicit encoder. Does not control the dictionary variable.

candidates.differentiation_notes: The controlled experiment design (hold pre-trained dictionary constant, vary only the encoder algorithm) has not been done. MP-SAE is closest but jointly trains both components.

candidates.title: Feature Absorption as Competitive Exclusion: Phase Transitions and Limiting Similarity

collisions.paper: No direct prior work applying competitive exclusion to SAE feature absorption.
collisions.overlap: General biology-ML analogies exist but competitive exclusion has never been applied to SAE absorption

collisions.paper: Tonolo et al., 2025. Generalized Lotka-Volterra with Sparse Interactions. arXiv:2503.20887
collisions.overlap: Studies LV models with sparse interactions and phase transitions. Ecology paper, no SAE connection.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Genuinely novel ecological framing. Pilot data supports the framework: different hierarchies show different absorption rates, consistent with different niche overlap distributions. Risk: may be evocative but not quantitatively productive.

candidates.title: Absorption-Aware Post-Hoc Feature Correction

collisions.paper: Wright & Sharkey, 2024. Per-latent scaling correction.
collisions.overlap: Corrects L1 shrinkage magnitude, not absorption-specific false negatives.

collisions.paper: Rajamanoharan et al., 2024. Gated SAE. arXiv:2404.16014
collisions.overlap: Training-time architecture, not post-hoc correction.

candidates.differentiation_notes: No prior work attempts post-hoc absorption correction at inference time. Practical contribution if it recovers meaningful fraction of SAE-vs-probe gap.

dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: REFUTED by pilot: Spearman rho=0.12 (target 0.6, failure threshold 0.3). Retained as negative result.
NOT SUPPORTED by pilot: rho=0.044 (p=0.83). Demoted to appendix.
Quantitative predictions not supported. Retained as qualitative framework.
ITAC achieves 3% vs 20% target. Merged into negative results.
Crowded by Muchane et al., Luo et al. HSAE, KronSAE.
Novelty 5/10. Qualitative trends known from SAEBench.

field_velocity_warning: SAE absorption is a fast-moving research area. Multiple groups are actively working on hierarchical SAE architectures (HSAE Feb 2026, Masked Regularization Apr 2026, KronSAE May 2025, OrtSAE Sep 2025, Matryoshka Mar 2025, MP-SAE Jun 2025). However, these focus on MITIGATION through architecture, not on CHARACTERIZATION across domains or CAUSAL MECHANISM CONFIRMATION. The proposal's characterization niche remains uncontested. Execution speed is critical.
changes_from_previous_report: GAS contribution removed (REFUTED, rho=0.12) -- moved to negative results
CMI contribution removed (NOT SUPPORTED, rho=0.044) -- moved to negative results
NEW contribution: Causal absorption via activation patching (8/10)
Architecture comparison elevated as standalone contribution (7/10)
Added cand_absorption_aware_correction as new backup candidate
Field velocity: Masked Regularization (arXiv:2604.06495, Apr 2026) is newest relevant paper -- mitigation not characterization
